rulings.Case Title: DANIEL PIERINI VS. COSMOPOLITAN PRIVATE SECURITY, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: AMENDED MOTION FOR SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, December 1, 2011, Line 1, PLAINTIFF DANIEL PIERINI's AMENDED MOTION FOR SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT. Denied. Moving party failed to provide courtesy copies of the moving papers as required by San Francisco Local Rule 2.6B. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: WELLS FARGO BANK N.A. VS. BRIAN K. HILLIARD et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Cross Defendant Wells Fargo Bank N.A. Demurrer To 2nd Amended Cross Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, December 1, 2011, Line 14, Cross Defendant Wells Fargo Bank N.A.'s Demurrer To 2nd Amended Cross Complaint. Sustained with ten days leave to amend as to all three claims. The claims are currently insufficient per Holcomb. Mr. Hilliard is granted leave to amend, if he can do in good faith, to allege; 1) negligent handling of the check he deposited with Wells Fargo separate and apart from any claim for negligent misrepresentation; 2) whether the alleged contract was written, oral, or implied by conduct and the specific terms of the contract that were breached; and 3) a misrepresentation and scienter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: ANGELICA M. GONZALEZ VS. ALFONSO PICAZO, AS TRUSTEES OF THE ARTHUR AND et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Motion To Compel The Attendance At Deposition And Production Of Documents By Defts, Production Of Documents, And Request For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, December 1, 2011, Line 3, PLAINTIFF ANGELICA GONZALEZ' Notice Of Hearing On Motion To Compel The Attendance At Deposition And Production Of Documents By Defts, Production Of Documents, And Request For Monetary Sanctions - The hearing is continued to December 5, 2011 at 9am The court issues the following tentative ruling in advance of the December 5 hearing. Granted. Both depositions are to be completed prior to December 13, 2011. The dates set forth in plaintiff¿s reply memorandum are reasonable. Although normally counsel should be able to schedule depositions without need for a motion, here neither side acted without substantial justification and thus all requests for sanctions are denied. Any party who contests this tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm on December 2 stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/HK

rulings.Case Title: TINA SOBERANIS, EXECUTOR OF THE ESTATE OF VS. KATHERINE GRIMME et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, December 1, 2011, Line 10, DEFENDANTs KATHERINE GRIMME, JOE CASTELLANO DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Overruled with ten days to answer. The complaint clearly alleges that defendants take the position that the August 5 agreement is not valid and the memorandum of agreement and non-partition agreement is at least arguably premised on the August 5 agreement. Therefore, it is permissible alternate pleading for plaintiff to allege that, if the August 5 agreement is invalid as contended by defendants, then there is no contractual bar to partition. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/HEK)

Plaintiffs adequately pled malice, fraud or oppression in connection with their second cause of action. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: PAUL STEPHENS et al VS. FIREMAN'S FUND INSURANCE COMPANY, A CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Fireman'S Fund Insurance Company'S Demurrer To Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, December 1, 2011, Line 16, Defendant Fireman'S Fund Insurance Company'S Demurrer To Complaint. Sustained with ten days leave to amend as to the fourth cause of action to plead whether the contract was written, oral, or implied and overruled as to all other causes of action. Plaintiffs do not attempt to state a cause of action for unfair insurance practices under Insurance Code 790.03(h); rather, they predicate the UCL claim on defendant¿s alleged violation of Civil Code 2860. The other causes of action are properly pled. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests.
